description:
You are an IT-support technician who has received a request from management:

“Tell us how many help-desk tickets are currently OPEN and still UNASSIGNED.  
Put that single number in a little text file we can read later.”

Inside your home directory you will find a SQLite database file located at  
/home/user/helpdesk.db

Schema of /home/user/helpdesk.db
• tickets
    • id          INTEGER  PRIMARY KEY
    • status      TEXT     -- values are ‘open’, ‘closed’, etc.
    • assigned_to TEXT     -- user name of technician or the word ‘unassigned’

What you must do
1. Query the database so you know how many rows in table tickets satisfy:
      status      = 'open'
      assigned_to = 'unassigned'

2. Create a brand-new text file called  
   /home/user/open_unassigned_count.log

3. Write exactly one line to that file, using the following strict format so automated tests can parse it:

   OPEN_UNASSIGNED_COUNT=<number>

   • There must be NO extra spaces before or after the ‘=’.
   • The line must end with a single UNIX newline “\n”.
   • Nothing else should appear in the file.

Example (DON’T hard-code this; your number will depend on the database contents):
OPEN_UNASSIGNED_COUNT=4

Hints
• You only need a single SQLite CLI command; no script files are required.
• Remember to execute the command from /home/user or give sqlite3 the full path to helpdesk.db.
• Verify the file content with cat before finishing.

The automated checker will:
a) open /home/user/open_unassigned_count.log  
b) confirm there is exactly one line, formatted as shown above, and  
c) ensure the numeric value matches the live database query described in step 1.

When you are done, the presence and contents of /home/user/open_unassigned_count.log must satisfy those rules.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts
-------------------------------------
1. File /home/user/helpdesk.db already exists.
2. The tickets table contains exactly the following six rows:

id | status | assigned_to
---+--------+-------------
1  | open   | unassigned
2  | closed | alice
3  | open   | unassigned
4  | open   | bob
5  | closed | unassigned
6  | open   | charlie

Therefore the correct count the agent must write is 2.

Expected file after the agent finishes
--------------------------------------
Path: /home/user/open_unassigned_count.log
Permissions: 0644 (rw-r--r--)
Content (including trailing newline):
OPEN_UNASSIGNED_COUNT=2\n
name: task_000000_6d0aef24
